statement.headerDoc:
STEP 992 — Brocard's problem: structural analysis
  ==================================================

  Follow-up to STEP 980 (n ∈ [13, 20]) and STEP 990 (n ∈ [21, 50]).
  Pivots from "extended verification range" to "structural understanding":
  why are n=4, 5, 7 the only known solutions?

  ★ Brocard 1876 / Ramanujan 1913 — OPEN 150 years ★
    The only known (n, m) with n! + 1 = m² are:
      n=4 → m=5   (25 = 5²)
      n=5 → m=11  (121 = 11²)
      n=7 → m=71  (5041 = 71²)
    Berndt–Galway 2000 verified no other solution for N ≤ 10⁹.

  ★ This file provides ★:
    Part 1: Known 3 soluti
